iterations.theme: From Sarajevo to NBA Stardom and Retirement: The Playing Career of Predrag Danilović
iterations.reason_for_cut: This iteration covers the entirety of Predrag Danilović's active playing career, ending precisely when he announces his retirement from professional basketball.

chapters.topic: Introduction of Predrag Saša Danilović
Early life and late start in basketball in Sarajevo
Childhood in Sarajevo and joining KK Bosna
Partizan fan and decision to leave Bosna for Partizan
The 'escape' to Partizan at 16 and Dule Vujošević's role
Two-year ban and studying English in America
Partizan's historic 1992 European Championship amidst war
Acknowledging Sale Đorđević's iconic shot
Joining the Yugoslav National Team with legends
Strength of the Yugoslav League and European dominance
Record of four European Championships with the national team
Dealing with injuries and the fear of failure
Transition to professional career, financial aspects, and move to Virtus Bologna
Choosing Virtus Bologna over more lucrative offers
Importance of point guard compatibility (Brunamonti, Đorđević)
Unspoken understanding and sacrifice with Sale Đorđević
Adjusting to fame and wealth in Bologna
The 'Cold Emperor' persona and its origin (media perception)
Recalling the famous clutch shot against Olympiacos
NBA draft, financial security, and European players in the NBA
Facing Michael Jordan and his unstoppable nature
Return to Virtus Bologna after NBA stint
Early retirement at 30, driven by physical and mental state
Relationship with Dražen Petrović and Toni Kukoč

iterations.theme: From Court to Boardroom: Leadership and Legacy in Serbian Basketball
iterations.reason_for_cut: This iteration marks a clear thematic shift from Danilović's playing career to his post-playing career, focusing on his administrative roles and broader reflections on basketball and life.

chapters.topic: Transition to management roles (Partizan, KSS)
Stepping out of comfort zone into administrative work
Learning the ropes of club management (Partizan's success)
Role as President of the Basketball Federation of Serbia (KSS)
Changes in player mentality and national team commitment (Luka Dončić example)
Hopes for the Serbian national team at the Olympics (Jokić, Bogdanović)
Final reflection: Was it all worth it?
